html:
Exclusive to:

Beefy Greens is a Food Item exclusive to Don't Starve Together introduced in Return of Them. It is cooked in a Crock Pot by combining at least 1 Leafy Meat and a Vegetable value of at least 3.0. It has a Crock Pot Priority of 25 and it takes 40 seconds to cook.

It restores a large amount of both Health and Hunger, making it a rather efficient way to use excess Vegetables if a reliable source of Leafy Meat is accessible, such as Lureplants. It notably has the same stats as Dragonpie, but spoils much faster.

While several character quotes suggest it is intended to be a salad, it is internally considered a Meat item. As such, Wigfrid can eat it, Wurt cannot, and Bunnymen will be hostile to anyone carrying one.

Recipe

≥3.0

Examples

×3

×3

Tips

  • Including Kelp Fronds or any Mushrooms will not result in Beefy Greens as they only provide a vegetable value of 0.5 each.

Trivia

  • Beefy Greens were introduced in the Troubled Waters update.
By TypeBy Crock Pot Value

–Warly

–Wormwood
“Huh. Pretty hearty for some leafy greens.”
–Winona
“Hyuyuyu! What a deliciously deceptive prank!”
–Wortox
“Don't think this real veggies!”
–Wurt
“What are those chunks in it?”
–Walter
“I found a stick in it... or at least I hope it's a stick.”
–Wanda

It restores a large amount of both Health and Hunger, making it a rather efficient way to use excess Vegetables if a reliable source of Leafy Meat is accessible, such as Lureplants. It notably has the same stats as Dragonpie, but spoils much faster.

'''Beefy Greens''' is a [[Food]] [[Items|Item]] exclusive to ''[[Don't Starve Together]]'' introduced in ''[[Return of Them]]''. It is cooked in a [[Crock Pot]] by combining at least 1 [[Leafy Meat]] and a [[Vegetable]] value of at least 3.0. It has a Crock Pot Priority of 25 and it takes 40 seconds to cook.

It restores a large amount of both Health and Hunger, making it a rather efficient way to use excess Vegetables if a reliable source of Leafy Meat is accessible, such as [[Lureplant]]s. It notably has the same stats as [[Dragonpie]], but spoils much faster.

While several character quotes suggest it is intended to be a salad, it is internally considered a Meat item. As such, [[Wigfrid]] can eat it, [[Wurt]] cannot, and [[Bunnymen]] will be hostile to anyone carrying one.

== {{pic|32|Cookbook}} Recipe ==
{{Recipe
|item1 = Leafy Meats
|item2 = Vegetables
|comparator2 = ≥
|count2 = 3.0
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Beefy Greens
}}

|item2 = Carrot
|count2 = 3
|tool = Crock Pot
|result = Beefy Greens}}

{{clear}}

== {{Pic|32|Prototype}} Tips ==
* Including [[Kelp Fronds]] or any [[Mushrooms]] will not result in Beefy Greens as they only provide a vegetable value of 0.5 each.

== {{Pic|32|Placeholder}} Trivia ==
* Beefy Greens were introduced in the ''[[Return of Them#Troubled Waters|Troubled Waters]]'' update.
